# Worked example — one match setup + three plies + settlement

**Every hash below is real.** This file is generated by `gen_sample_exchange.py`, which uses only
the reference constructions in `verify_vectors.py`. Re-run the script to regenerate it; reproduce
the hashes with your own implementation to check your conformance on a realistic flow.

Scenario: sub-game 0 of a Mode A (full-disclosure) match on a 10x10 board. Team-Aleph is the cop,
Team-Bet is the thief (thief moves first, so the thief owns even plies).

## 1. The match card (SPEC §4.1)

```json
{
  "protocol": "league/0.1",
  "match_id": "2026-08-01-aleph-vs-bet",
  "groups": {
    "group_1": "Team-Aleph",
    "group_2": "Team-Bet"
  },
  "urls": {
    "group_1_cop": "https://aleph.example/cop/mcp",
    "group_1_thief": "https://aleph.example/thief/mcp",
    "group_2_cop": "https://bet.example/cop/mcp",
    "group_2_thief": "https://bet.example/thief/mcp"
  },
  "grid": [
    10,
    10
  ],
  "rounds": 25,
  "num_games": 6,
  "swap_at": 3,
  "max_barriers": 0,
  "disclosure": "A",
  "scent_k": null,
  "stage": "demo",
  "report_email": "league-reports@example.com",
  "seed": "league-spec-v0.1-example",
  "scheduled_utc": "2026-08-01T18:00:00Z",
  "timeouts": {
    "per_ply_seconds": 120,
    "per_subgame_seconds": 1800,
    "max_messages": 200
  }
}
```

Canonical hash of the card (SPEC §2) — both sides must compute the same value:

```
config_sha256 = 568ac8e895f38e67cac1abf563e20baec2f05a9ff09bd28b062fa896757657ac
```

## 2. Start cells from the seed (SPEC §6.4)

`derive_starts("league-spec-v0.1-example", index=0, n=10)` gives:

```
cop start   = [0, 4]
thief start = [5, 0]
```

## 3. Ply-zero handshake (SPEC §4.3)

Team-Aleph -> Team-Bet's thief mailbox (prose + trailer, one `deliver_message` string):

```
Hello Team-Bet! Aleph here, confirming we are ready to start series
2026-08-01-aleph-vs-bet as agreed. Our trailer carries the protocol
version and our hash of the match card - please verify it matches yours.
---LEAGUE-v1---
{"v":1,"hello":{"protocol":"league/0.1","config_sha256":"568ac8e895f38e67cac1abf563e20baec2f05a9ff09bd28b062fa896757657ac","group":"Team-Aleph"}}
```

Team-Bet replies symmetrically:

```
---LEAGUE-v1---
{"v":1,"hello":{"protocol":"league/0.1","config_sha256":"568ac8e895f38e67cac1abf563e20baec2f05a9ff09bd28b062fa896757657ac","group":"Team-Bet"}}
```

Both `config_sha256` values match -> the match may begin. If they differ, nothing starts.

## 4. Three in-game plies (SPEC §5, Mode A)

### Ply 0 — Team-Bet (thief) moves to `[6, 0]`

```
I'm on the move. You'll have to do better than waiting in a corner, officer.
My move and commitment are below, as per the league protocol.
---LEAGUE-v1---
{"v":1,"game":0,"ply":0,"ack":-1,"move":[6,0],"commit":"f1517431a09c03e8626ed8816d62175eec683f441d7f4065501b6ffb4366b7fa","nonce":"a1b2c3d4e5f60718","reveal":null,"state":"5f4a5b18b67c6959f4f9ea30228bb5c80b93558f87d5a88c1ed3d4216315d07a"}
```

Receiver's checks (SPEC §8.1): trailer parses -> `game`/`ply` are the expected next block ->
`commit == sha256_canonical({"nonce":"a1b2c3d4e5f60718","pos":[6, 0]})` = `f1517431a09c03e8...` ✓ ->
apply `[6, 0]` to the local replica -> recompute
`state_hash([], "cop", 1)` = `5f4a5b18b67c6959...` and compare with the trailer's `state` ✓.

### Ply 1 — Team-Aleph (cop) moves to `[0, 5]`

```
Bold words for someone leaving footprints. I'm sweeping toward the center
to cut off your escape lanes. Move block below.
---LEAGUE-v1---
{"v":1,"game":0,"ply":1,"ack":0,"move":[0,5],"commit":"13913cb90de3dfa257187462b36f80d3fbd149127bf1d8fb6c56a64f9669fddc","nonce":"00112233445566778899aabbccddeeff","reveal":null,"state":"320472f419c308e9342ab5471b74648de0b407d675188badb8cc7ee1f77e4606"}
```

Receiver's checks (SPEC §8.1): trailer parses -> `game`/`ply` are the expected next block ->
`commit == sha256_canonical({"nonce":"00112233445566778899aabbccddeeff","pos":[0, 5]})` = `13913cb90de3dfa2...` ✓ ->
apply `[0, 5]` to the local replica -> recompute
`state_hash([], "thief", 2)` = `320472f419c308e9...` and compare with the trailer's `state` ✓.

### Ply 2 — Team-Bet (thief) moves to `[7, 1]`

```
Center, you say? Then you won't mind that I'm heading somewhere else
entirely. Committed below.
---LEAGUE-v1---
{"v":1,"game":0,"ply":2,"ack":1,"move":[7,1],"commit":"341735efcd59c720a9c0f8ca5e12ead116d3c21395e8db16cb2e6161f35c7598","nonce":"deadbeef00c0ffee","reveal":null,"state":"9e6aa3ee46d19c0dc7a9214c2291ee20593e722089c329ffda1d3b1e004e4158"}
```

Receiver's checks (SPEC §8.1): trailer parses -> `game`/`ply` are the expected next block ->
`commit == sha256_canonical({"nonce":"deadbeef00c0ffee","pos":[7, 1]})` = `341735efcd59c720...` ✓ ->
apply `[7, 1]` to the local replica -> recompute
`state_hash([], "cop", 3)` = `9e6aa3ee46d19c0d...` and compare with the trailer's `state` ✓.

## 5. Settlement (SPEC §9)

After the last sub-game each side builds its report from its own log, canonicalizes, hashes, and
exchanges only the hash. E.g. for an (illustrative, schema-TBD) report body
`{"report_type":"bonus_game","totals_by_group":{"Team-A":80,"Team-B":60}}` both sides would send:

```
REPORT_SHA:e8f42c20d33a961f495da0c8051e5e99c829a63cb1d36355bdb3f0df8b746170
```

Byte-identical hashes -> both teams email the same report. Any mismatch -> the escalation in
SPEC §9 step 4. Nothing is ever emailed without a confirmed match.

## Notes for implementers (and their LLM assistants)

- The prose above the fence is interpreted by the receiving agent's LLM; it may bluff (the thief
  lies at ply 2). The trailer may not: it is constructed programmatically, and the receiver
  verifies it mechanically without any LLM involvement.
- `move_count` passed to `state_hash` is plies *completed* (ply 0 -> 1, ply 1 -> 2, ...), and
  `turn` is the role to move *next* — that's why ply 0's state frame is `("cop", 1)`.
- In this example barriers are disabled (`max_barriers: 0`), so `barriers` is always `[]` in the
  state frame.
